worth_it_explanation: Pre-bid fraud avoidance through DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab is a distinct capability from post-bid measurement: it stops the bid before the impression is bought rather than reporting on fraudulent impressions after the spend is gone. An account that has DoubleVerify active for post-bid measurement but has not activated pre-bid avoidance segments in the DSP is paying for invalid traffic it could have blocked at the auction. These checks confirm that pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are active, that their stringency aligns with what post-bid measurement is actually finding, and that coverage is consistent across campaigns and DSPs rather than applied to some lines while others run unprotected.
intro: DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab offers two distinct modes for addressing invalid traffic (IVT). The first is post-bid measurement: a verification tag fires on delivered impressions and classifies each one as valid, general invalid traffic (GIVT), or sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT). The second is pre-bid avoidance: a segment activated inside the DSP before the bid is placed, so the platform never enters auctions for impressions the Fraud Lab's models would flag. These two modes address the same problem at different points in the buying cycle, and they are not substitutes for each other. A pre-bid segment reduces spend against fraudulent inventory; a post-bid tag measures what got through. Both are needed for a complete fraud posture, and each must be configured to match the other for the combination to make sense.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are not applied in the DSP, so the account relies on post-bid measurement alone
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are absent from DSP line items, the Fraud Lab cannot stop the bid before it reaches fraudulent inventory. The account enters auctions for invalid impressions and is billed for delivery before any measurement occurs. Post-bid measurement documents the exposure, but the spend is already gone by the time the Fraud Lab classifies those impressions as invalid.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab provides pre-bid avoidance through segments that DSPs activate at the line-item level before submitting bids. When a pre-bid segment is active, the DSP's bidder evaluates each impression opportunity against the Fraud Lab's fraud and invalid traffic (IVT) models before deciding whether to bid. Impressions that the Fraud Lab flags as high-risk for GIVT or sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) are excluded from the auction before the bid is placed and before any cost is incurred.

Without a pre-bid segment, the DSP bids on all available inventory, including the impressions the Fraud Lab would have filtered out. Post-bid measurement runs after delivery and classifies what was bought, but the billing has already happened. The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines GIVT and SIVT as distinct IVT categories, and the Fraud Lab is MRC-accredited for SIVT detection across desktop, mobile web, mobile in-app, and connected TV (CTV) environments. That accreditation describes detection capability; it does not block fraud on its own. Blocking requires the pre-bid segment to be active in the DSP.

Pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are configured separately from the post-bid measurement tag. A line item can carry both a DV measurement tag (for post-bid reporting) and a pre-bid segment (for avoidance before the bid). Many line items are set up with only the measurement tag because measurement was the initial integration goal and the pre-bid segment was not added at setup time.

A few signs point to pre-bid avoidance not being active:
  • DV Pinnacle's Fraud Lab shows IVT findings against active campaigns, but no pre-bid segment appears in the targeting settings of the corresponding DSP line items
  • The DSP line item's brand safety or fraud targeting section shows a DV measurement tag but no exclusion based on a DV pre-bid fraud segment
  • The DoubleVerify account team confirms the integration is configured for verification only, with no pre-bid avoidance product activated
You may want to verify pre-bid segment status in the DSP directly rather than relying on the DV Pinnacle summary. Both modes generate data in the Fraud Lab and the dashboard alone does not distinguish them.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, fraudulent impressions on display or video line items consume budget without generating any shopping signal or conversion data. Pre-bid avoidance reduces the volume of IVT entering the buying pool, so the engagement and conversion metrics that feed line-item optimization reflect activity from real users rather than invalid traffic.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, post-bid IVT findings confirm that fraudulent impressions were bought, but do not recover the spend. Pre-bid avoidance reduces the share of budget reaching invalid inventory before billing, so the cost-per-lead calculation is not inflated by impressions that had no chance of producing a form submission.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
Brand suitability in Display and Video 360

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle documentation covering pre-bid fraud avoidance segment setup and DSP activation requires a partner login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team or log in to DV Pinnacle directly for activation guides specific to your DSP integrations.

impactful_updates.update_title: DoubleVerify updates standard verification pre-bid rate card (April 2025)
impactful_updates.update_description: Starting April 2025, DoubleVerify updated its standard verification pre-bid rate card for Display and Video 360 (DV360), with marginally higher CPM rates for some solutions. Advertisers with custom negotiated rates are not affected.

investigate.intro: Confirm, for each active DSP line item running with DoubleVerify fraud measurement, whether a pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is also applied as a targeting exclusion, or whether only a post-bid measurement tag is present.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Review which campaigns and line items are generating fraud measurement data.
For each active campaign shown in Fraud Lab, note the campaign name and the DSP it is running in. This is the list you will cross-reference against the DSP's targeting settings.

In DV Pinnacle, confirm whether a pre-bid avoidance product is shown as active for each advertiser or campaign. The DV Pinnacle interface distinguishes between verification (post-bid) and avoidance (pre-bid) configurations; note which mode is active for each.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether a DoubleVerify pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is applied in the DSP targeting settings alongside or instead of only a post-bid measurement tag. A line item with a pre-bid avoidance segment actively applied passes. A line item with only a post-bid measurement tag and no pre-bid avoidance segment is a fail, recorded with the line item name, the DSP, and the estimated spend running on post-bid measurement only.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the DSP targeting configuration for each active line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DSP line items with DoubleVerify fraud measurement are not paired with pre-bid fraud avoidance segments. The account is running post-bid measurement through the Fraud Lab, which classifies invalid traffic (IVT) after impressions are delivered and billed, but the pre-bid segment that would stop the bid before reaching fraudulent inventory is not applied in the DSP. Every IVT impression the Fraud Lab records represents spend that had already cleared billing before measurement ran. {{voice_recommend}} confirming with the DoubleVerify account team whether the pre-bid avoidance product is activated on the account, then applying the appropriate pre-bid fraud avoidance segment as a targeting exclusion on each affected line item so the DSP bidder filters fraudulent impression opportunities before the bid, not after.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid fraud avoidance stringency does not match post-bid findings, so avoidance and measurement produce inconsistent signals
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the pre-bid avoidance segment is set to a different stringency level than what post-bid measurement is tracking, the account cannot use Fraud Lab findings to evaluate whether avoidance is working. A looser pre-bid threshold lets through impressions that post-bid measurement will flag, while a more restrictive threshold blocks inventory that would have passed post-bid review. The mismatch makes it impossible to close the loop between what was blocked and what was measured.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify's pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are tiered. Each tier represents a different stringency level: the most permissive tiers block only the clearest IVT signals, while more restrictive tiers expand the set of impression characteristics that trigger an exclusion. Post-bid measurement independently classifies delivered impressions using the Fraud Lab's detection models and reports a GIVT and SIVT rate for what was actually bought.

For pre-bid avoidance to reduce post-bid IVT findings, the pre-bid segment's stringency must be set to catch the kinds of IVT the post-bid measurement is actually detecting. If the pre-bid segment is set to a low stringency tier and post-bid measurement consistently shows SIVT findings, the pre-bid segment is not covering the fraud type that is reaching the account. The IVT that post-bid identifies was not blocked before the bid because the pre-bid threshold was not set to catch it.

The reverse also creates a problem. A pre-bid segment set to maximum stringency blocks a wider range of inventory, including placements the post-bid measurement would have classified as valid. That additional exclusion reduces reach beyond what the account's actual fraud findings would require, raising cost per thousand impressions (CPM) without a corresponding reduction in post-bid IVT.

Alignment between pre-bid stringency and post-bid findings allows the account to evaluate whether avoidance is working: post-bid IVT rates should be lower on line items running pre-bid avoidance than on comparable line items without it, and the types of IVT caught post-bid should not be the same as what the pre-bid segment is configured to block.

A few signs point to a stringency mismatch:
  • Post-bid IVT findings in the Fraud Lab show SIVT types that the pre-bid segment's documented tier is not designed to catch
  • Post-bid IVT rates on line items with pre-bid avoidance active are not lower than rates on similar line items without it
  • The pre-bid segment tier was set at campaign launch and has not been reviewed against Fraud Lab findings since then
You may want to compare post-bid IVT findings by type (GIVT versus SIVT) against the pre-bid segment's tier definition before adjusting stringency. GIVT and SIVT require different prevention approaches.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running video and CTV campaigns, SIVT on those formats is more expensive per invalid impression than on display. You may want to confirm that the pre-bid fraud segment applied to video and CTV line items is set to at least the same stringency as the SIVT types the Fraud Lab is detecting post-bid on those formats.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser with in-app mobile line items, SIVT types specific to mobile environments, such as app bundle spoofing or SDK fraud, may require a higher pre-bid stringency tier than the default. You may want to review whether the pre-bid segment tier in use covers the SIVT subtypes appearing in post-bid Fraud Lab findings for mobile placements.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
What is the IAB Tech Lab Spiders and Bots list?

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle documentation describing the specific IVT types covered at each pre-bid stringency tier requires a partner login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team for the tier-by-tier coverage specification relevant to your DSP integrations.

investigate.intro: Compare the pre-bid fraud avoidance segment tier applied to each line item against the post-bid IVT findings from the Fraud Lab, and confirm whether the stringency level matches the types of IVT the account is actually receiving.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Pull the IVT breakdown for each active campaign, separating GIVT from SIVT. Note the SIVT rate per campaign and which SIVT subtypes are appearing most frequently.

In DV Pinnacle, locate the pre-bid avoidance configuration for each advertiser or campaign. Note the stringency tier or product name applied to each. If DV Pinnacle shows a pre-bid avoidance tier label, record it alongside the corresponding post-bid SIVT findings.

Identify any campaign where the SIVT types detected post-bid are not covered by the pre-bid tier applied. This is the stringency gap. Record the campaign name, the pre-bid tier, and the SIVT type that is appearing in post-bid findings.

Also note any campaign where the pre-bid tier is set to a higher stringency than the post-bid findings would suggest is needed. Record the tier and confirm whether the additional exclusion is intentional or a default that was never reviewed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the pre-bid avoidance tier per campaign alongside the dominant IVT types appearing in post-bid Fraud Lab findings. A campaign where the pre-bid tier covers the IVT types detected post-bid, and where post-bid IVT rates on lines with pre-bid avoidance active are lower than on comparable lines without it, passes. A campaign where the same SIVT types are appearing post-bid that the pre-bid tier should have filtered is a fail, recorded with the campaign name, the pre-bid tier, and the SIVT type detected post-bid. A campaign where the pre-bid tier is set higher than post-bid findings justify is a note, recorded with the tier and an estimate of the reach impact.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid avoidance tier and the post-bid GIVT and SIVT rates per campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} a mismatch between the pre-bid fraud avoidance segment stringency applied to {{site_name}}'s DSP line items and the types of invalid traffic (IVT) that DoubleVerify's Fraud Lab is detecting in post-bid measurement. When the pre-bid tier is not set to cover the IVT types the Fraud Lab reports post-bid, the avoidance layer is not blocking the specific fraud patterns reaching the account, and the two measurement modes cannot be compared to evaluate whether avoidance is reducing exposure. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the post-bid SIVT breakdown by campaign in DV Pinnacle, comparing it against the pre-bid segment tier in use, and aligning the pre-bid stringency to cover the SIVT types the Fraud Lab is consistently detecting, then confirming over the next flight that post-bid IVT rates on those line items decrease.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are applied unevenly across campaigns or DSPs, leaving some lines exposed to invalid traffic
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When pre-bid fraud avoidance is applied to some DSP line items but not others, the campaigns without coverage absorb IVT that the protected campaigns avoid. Budget running on unprotected lines is exposed to the same invalid traffic pool that pre-bid avoidance on other lines is designed to exclude, and post-bid measurement on those lines will reflect that gap.

educate.base:
Pre-bid fraud avoidance segments must be applied at the line-item level in the DSP. There is no account-wide or campaign-wide setting that automatically extends pre-bid avoidance to every line item under an advertiser. Each line item is configured individually, and a line item created after the initial pre-bid setup is not automatically covered: it inherits default DSP settings, not the fraud avoidance configuration of existing line items.

Uneven coverage arises in a few common patterns. An advertiser may activate pre-bid avoidance on display line items when the integration is first set up and not extend it to video or connected TV (CTV) line items added later. A campaign built by a different team member or agency may not carry the pre-bid segment if there is no documented standard for line-item setup. A DSP integration activated for one buying entity may not extend to a second entity running spend for the same advertiser on a different DSP account.

The result is that post-bid IVT rates differ across line items not because the underlying inventory differs, but because some lines have pre-bid avoidance filtering out fraud before the bid while others are buying from the full inventory pool. Comparing post-bid IVT rates across line items without accounting for pre-bid coverage produces misleading conclusions about which supply sources are clean.

A few signs point to uneven pre-bid coverage:
  • DV Pinnacle's Fraud Lab shows materially different IVT rates across line items within the same insertion order or campaign without an obvious explanation related to format or targeting
  • Some line items appear in post-bid Fraud Lab reporting with high IVT rates while others running similar targeting show much lower rates
  • The DSP account has line items created at different times, and the older line items have a pre-bid segment in their targeting while newer ones do not
You may want to audit line-item targeting configurations in the DSP against the full active campaign list rather than relying on Fraud Lab reporting alone to identify coverage gaps. Lines without any DV tag will not appear in Fraud Lab at all.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running separate campaigns for prospecting and remarketing, the prospecting campaigns may have been set up with pre-bid fraud avoidance while remarketing campaigns added later were not. You may want to confirm pre-bid segment coverage on each campaign type separately. Both draw from inventory pools that can carry IVT.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser buying across multiple DSPs, pre-bid fraud avoidance may be active in one DSP but not connected or configured in a second. You may want to confirm that each DSP integration has the DoubleVerify pre-bid segment applied to line items in that platform, not only in the primary DSP where the integration was first set up.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Invalid Traffic Detection (IVT) Guidelines
Brand suitability in Display and Video 360

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle documentation for auditing pre-bid segment coverage across line items and DSPs requires a partner login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team to review coverage reports or request a pre-bid activation audit across your connected DSP accounts.

investigate.intro: Audit each active DSP line item to confirm whether a DoubleVerify pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is present in the targeting configuration, and identify any line items that are running without pre-bid coverage.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Fraud Lab section. Review the full list of campaigns and line items showing post-bid IVT data. Note any campaigns or line items that do not appear in Fraud Lab at all: those are running without any DV measurement active and are candidates for both missing post-bid tagging and missing pre-bid avoidance.

For the line items that do appear in Fraud Lab, compare post-bid IVT rates across line items within the same campaign or insertion order. A large difference in IVT rates across similar line items is a signal that pre-bid coverage may differ between them.
In DV Pinnacle, confirm which line items or campaigns have a pre-bid avoidance product active. Note the campaign name, the DSP, and whether pre-bid avoidance is shown as active or inactive for each.

Cross-reference the active line item list from each DSP against the DV Pinnacle pre-bid configuration to identify any line items that are running spend without a pre-bid fraud avoidance segment applied. Record each gap with the line item name, the DSP, and the format type.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether each active DSP line item with DoubleVerify fraud measurement also has a pre-bid fraud avoidance segment applied. A line item with both post-bid measurement and a pre-bid avoidance segment active passes. A line item with post-bid measurement but no pre-bid avoidance segment is a fail recorded with the line item name, the DSP, and the format type. A line item with no DV tag of any kind is also a fail, recorded separately as a monitoring coverage gap. Full pre-bid coverage means every active line item has a pre-bid segment applied and no line item runs on post-bid measurement only.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid avoidance status for each active line item and DSP above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are not applied consistently across {{site_name}}'s active DSP line items. Some campaigns have DoubleVerify pre-bid avoidance active and are filtering fraudulent impression opportunities before the bid, while others are running on post-bid measurement only and absorbing invalid traffic (IVT) that the pre-bid segment on protected lines is designed to exclude. The IVT rate difference between covered and uncovered lines reflects the gap in protection, not a difference in the underlying supply quality. {{voice_recommend}} auditing the targeting configuration of every active line item in each DSP, applying the DoubleVerify pre-bid fraud avoidance segment to any line item that currently carries only a post-bid measurement tag, and establishing a line-item setup standard that includes the pre-bid segment so new campaigns are protected from launch.
